mnemonics.title: P-C-AL Compilation Order
mnemonics.content: P-C-AL: Preprocess → Compile → Assemble → Link. Remember: 'Please Compile And Link' - The four stages of C++ compilation in order.

mnemonics.title: G++ Command Memory
mnemonics.content: g++ -o output source.cpp → 'g++ [output flag] [exename] [sourcefile]'. Remember: 'go output source' - Go create output from source.

mnemonics.title: Error Types Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: S-L-R: Syntax (Spelling), Linker (Libraries), Runtime (Resources). Remember: 'Sam Likes Reading' - Syntax, Linker, Runtime errors.

mnemonics.title: Preprocessor Pillars
mnemonics.content: #include, #define, #ifndef → 'Include Define If Not Defined'. These are the three main preprocessor directives to remember.

mnemonics.title: Object File Purpose
mnemonics.content: OBJ = Object Code Binary. Remember: 'Oh Be Good' - Object files are good intermediate outputs before linking.

acronyms.usage: Use: Command to compile C++ programs in Linux/Unix systems. Example: g++ program.cpp -o program
Use: The broader compiler suite that includes g++ for C++ compilation
Use: Software like Code::Blocks, Dev-C++ that combines editor, compiler, and debugger in one interface
Use: Intermediate file generated after assembly stage, contains machine code but not yet linked
Use: Final output file that can be run directly by the operating system
Use: Programming paradigm that C++ supports - classes, objects, inheritance, polymorphism

visualTips.visualization: Imagine water flowing through 4 filters: Source(.cpp) → Preprocessor(#include, #define) → Compiler(creates .s assembly) → Assembler(creates .obj) → Linker(creates .exe). Each stage transforms the code.
Think of the preprocessor as a copy-paste machine: It reads #include files and inserts them, replaces #define macros, then hands the expanded code to the compiler.
Picture the linker as a puzzle assembler: It takes multiple .obj pieces (from different source files) and connects function calls to their definitions, creating one complete .exe puzzle.
Visualize a building: Text segment (instructions) at bottom, Data segment (global vars) above it, Heap (dynamic memory) growing up, Stack (local variables) growing down.

analogies.analogy: Like a recipe book copier - before cooking, you gather all ingredients (header files) and their quantities (macros) from other pages and combine them into one master recipe.
Like a human translator converting English (C++ source) to machine code (binary) - it checks grammar (syntax) and converts meaning to a different language.
Like a book binder - takes chapters (object files), finds cross-references between them, and binds everything into one complete book (executable).
Like a grammatical mistake in English - 'I goes to store' - the sentence structure is wrong, compiler can't understand it.
Like a missing page in a book - when Chapter 3 references Chapter 5 but Chapter 5 doesn't exist, the binder (linker) fails to complete the book.
Like a car malfunction while driving - the car built correctly (compiled) but something goes wrong during execution (out of gas, flat tire = division by zero, null pointer).
